pattern: A single sort with a composite key — population count first, value as the tiebreaker — produces the required ordering without manual grouping.
hints: The ordering has two levels: how many set bits a number has, and then the number itself. A composite sort key captures both at once.
Counting set bits is just summing the binary digits; many languages also expose a bit-count primitive.
Sort by the pair (popcount(x), x). The popcount is the primary key and x breaks ties, so equal-popcount numbers come out in ascending value order.

edge_inputs.description: consecutive small integers spread across several popcounts
all single-bit values, so the tiebreaker fully orders them
duplicates with equal popcount keep all copies
zero must sort before everything else
larger value with fewer bits precedes smaller value with more bits
max-magnitude values mixed with small ones

stress.description: random values across the full range to exercise both sort keys at scale
stress.note: duplicates and equal-popcount ties occur naturally
